narrative.summary: You're climbing the stairs off Geary Ave into a second-floor wine bar built for dancing—low lighting, a dance floor that got upgraded after enough complaints, and a sound system that'll hit you in the chest when the headliner takes over. There's no quiet place to escape the bass, so the seating area sits at the same volume as the floor. Come for DJ nights when you want something looser than a club but more committed than a bar with a playlist.

blocks.title: The Space
blocks.body: You climb the stairs off Geary Ave—Toronto's industrial strip turned art-studio colony—and hit a second-floor room that's all minimalist intention: low lighting that got upgraded after enough complaints, a dance floor now properly lit for bodies in motion, and a back room they've opened up to handle overflow when the DJ pulls a crowd. The bar runs efficient, coat check moves fast, but there's nowhere to escape the bass when you need a breather—the seating area sits at the same volume as the floor.

blocks.title: The Food
blocks.body: This is a wine bar built for dancing, not dining. You're here for the sound system—which reviewers rate as acceptable, nothing audiophile-grade, though the bass hits hard when the headliner takes over—and whatever natural wine they're pouring that week. Expect small bites at best, the kind of thing you can eat standing up between sets.

blocks.title: Go When
blocks.body: You show up for DJ nights when you want something looser than a proper club but more committed than a bar with a playlist. Come early if you want to actually talk, stay late when you've stopped caring about conversation.

blocks.title: The Vibe
blocks.body:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Edit 2026: upgrading my review to five stars because they've added extra lighting on the dance floor and improved the back room to add dancing space.

Great space, great sound, just needs someone working the lights.   I took this photo early in the night before it filled up, but throughout the night they just turned down and then off some of the lights.

The sound during the warm up was fine but then the headliner came on and then you then heard how much more bass the speakers can put out."

⭐⭐⭐⭐ "I liked the minimalistic vibe and lighting. Good coat check, entry, and bar experience. I agree with other reviewers that the sound system is acceptable — but nothing special. My gripe is that there is no quiet place to wind down. The seating area away from the dance floor is just as loud. Imagine how much better this place would be if it had a chill area where you could escape the relentless undst-undst-undst of the dance floor and reconnect with your friends."

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Had an amazing time at this club! The lights are awesome, the sound was awesome and DJ Benski was incredible and kept the good vibes going all night!"
